incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly on first attempt

steps.text: Check the data table. Match each bar to its number.

incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly on second attempt

steps.text: Let's think about this together. Tomatoes need to reach 60. Use the gridlines to help you count by 5s.

incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets all bars to correct heights: Tomatoes=60, Peppers=35, Carrots=45, Beans=20.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I look at the table: Tomatoes is 60, so I count by 5s on the gridlines to 60. Peppers is 35, count to 35. Carrots is 45, count to 45. Beans is 20, count to 20. There we go.

incorrects.description: Student selected 70: miscounted or used wrong categories

steps.text: Here's a hint: Peppers is 35 and Carrots is 45. Add those two numbers together.

incorrects.description: Student selected 75: calculation error or estimation

steps.text: You're working on it. Check the bars carefully. Peppers ends at 35, Carrots at 45. What's 35 plus 45?

incorrects.description: Student selected 90: added an extra category or doubled one value

steps.text: Let's think about this a bit more. We only need Peppers and Carrots. Don't include the other plants.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System shows 35 + 45 = 80 with visual annotation on the two bars.

steps.text: Let me walk you through this one. Peppers is 35 plants. Carrots is 45 plants. I add 35 plus 45, which equals 80. The answer is 80 plants in all. See how that works?
